# Define a function to format the output
def format_output(output):
    # Split the output into lines
    lines = output.split('\n')
    
    # Create a list to store the formatted lines
    formatted_lines = []
    
    # Iterate over each line
    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
        # Check if the line is a title line
        if line.startswith('1. **'):
            # Extract the title and attraction
            title = line[3:].split(': ')[0]
            attraction = line[3:].split(': ')[1]
            
            # Format the title and attraction
            formatted_title = f'**{i+1}. {title} - {attraction}**'
            formatted_lines.append(formatted_title)
        else:
            # Add the line to the formatted lines
            formatted_lines.append(line)
    
    # Join the formatted lines back into a string
    formatted_output = '\n\n'.join(formatted_lines)
    
    return formatted_output

# Format the output
formatted_output = format_output(output)

# Print the formatted output
